LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BEREHANU CHALLA'S DEMURRER TO HERMAN LEDBETTER'S AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ALLOWED ORAL ARGUMENT AS DEFENDANT INDICATED THAT HE HAD COMPLIED WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING RULE (RE GIVING PROPER NOTICE TO OPPOSING PARTY OF HIS INTENT TO APPEAR AT THE HEARING). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ALLEGE HOW PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN DAMAGED BY THE ALLEGED FRAUD. 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. OVERRULED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES A DUTY TO EXERCISE REASONABLE CARE AND A BREACH THEREOF. PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES ARE A QUESTION OF FACT, NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO DEMURRER AND NEITHER BUDD NOR HOLLOWAY SUPPORT THE GRANTING OF THE DEMURRER. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER IF PLAINTIFF CHOOSES NOT TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BEREHANU CHALLA'S DEMURRER TO HERMAN LEDBETTER'S AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Berehanu H. Challa, Esq. of Law Offices of Berehanu H. Challa 650/688-5786 deft & moving party; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court allowed oral argument as defendant indicated that he had complied with the tentative ruling rule (re giving proper notice to opposing party of his intent to appear at the hearing). Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ALLEGE HOW PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN DAMAGED BY THE ALLEGED FRAUD. 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. OVERRULED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES A DUTY TO EXERCISE REASONABLE CARE AND A BREACH THEREOF. PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES ARE A QUESTION OF FACT, NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO DEMURRER AND NEITHER BUDD NOR HOLLOWAY SUPPORT THE GRANTING OF THE DEMURRER. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER IF PLAINTIFF CHOOSES NOT TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2007 Text Ruling
